Understanding Knowledge Graphs

Knowledge graphs represent structured information as a network of entities and relationships. They have become fundamental to modern search engines and AI systems.

Core Concepts

At their heart, knowledge graphs consist of nodes (entities) connected by edges (relationships). Each node can have properties, and each edge defines how two nodes relate.

Applications

Knowledge graphs power recommendation systems, semantic search, and question answering. Google's Knowledge Graph, introduced in 2012, was one of the first large-scale deployments.
